D5 Design L2 Understand Reference

Contrast of Pure Hue

Itten's first contrast — undiluted primaries clash hardest; drain the saturation and the energy drains with it

The contrast of hue is strongest among the three primaries at full saturation — red, yellow, blue side by side. Add more hues and it softens; desaturate and it collapses toward grey. Pure-hue contrast needs purity.

Choose how many pure hues sit side by side, then drain their saturation. Watch the vivid clash of the primary triad fade into a quiet grey field — the contrast lives entirely in the purity of the hues.
